name: chain_built_a_selfhealing_error_system_that_w
method: Built a self-healing error system that watches my prod logs, launches Claude to fix bugs, and I approve fixes from Telegram I run a couple of Node.js services and got really tired of constantly checki

stages.task: Tail app logs for all surge/vercel deploys. Fingerprint each error: sha256(message + ''.join(stack[:3])). Deduplicate — only surface each unique fingerprint once per hour. Write new errors to error_queue.json.
For each error in queue: read relevant source file, call claude -p with error context + code snippet, request unified diff patch. Store proposed fix with confidence score.
Severity < 2 (non-breaking): auto-approve. Severity >= 2: send Telegram message with error summary + proposed diff. Wait up to 30 min for APPROVE/REJECT reply. Timeout = auto-reject.
Apply approved patch via git apply. Redeploy via surge CLI or vercel CLI. Smoke-test the URL (HTTP 200 check). Log result to fix_log.jsonl. If deploy fails, rollback and alert.
